name: Jook, Chinese Thick Rice Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the rice 2 or 3 times, then soak overnight.
2. Mince the green onions and water chestnuts.
3. Mix minced ingredients with ground pork, dark soy sauce, and salt.
4. Combine stock and rice in a large pot.
5. Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce heat to low.
6. Simmer for 2-3 hours or until the rice breaks down completely and the soup thickens and becomes creamy.
7. Increase heat to medium and add the pork mixture, forming small balls about the size of a teaspoon and dropping them into the soup one at a time.
8. Cook the pork balls for an additional 5 minutes, or until they are fully cooked.
9. Adjust seasoning as needed.
10. Serve in individual soup bowls.
11. Pass condiments around for each person to add their favorite toppings (e.g., sesame oil, white pepper, chopped green onions, Chinese parsley, tea melon, Szechwan turnips, chopped peanuts, deep-fried devils).
12. This recipe can be made ahead of time and reheated as needed.
13. You may substitute chicken slices or ground beef for the pork; fish fillets or ham slices work well too. A roast chicken or turkey carcass is ideal for making stock.
14. Add duck liver sausage bits if desired.
(Note: From "Dim Sum" by Rhoda Yee, posted by James Lor.)
